description: Standard run: LinkedIn writer skill + 2 Level 1 criteria
prompt: Run auto-research on .claude/skills/content/SKILL.md. Use these two criteria: (1) Hook must be under 136 characters — Level 1. (2) Bullet points must use hyphens only — Level 1. Cap at 5 iterations.
expected_behavior: Reads references/learnings.md at startup
Reads the target skill file in full
Presents exactly the 2 specified criteria with Level 1 classification and waits for approval
Generates at least 5 test inputs and writes them to harness/test-inputs.md
Runs baseline 5 times before starting the loop
Logs baseline to results.md
Spawns test-runner sub-agent (not evaluating outputs itself) in each iteration
Spawns eval-deterministic sub-agent for Level 1 criteria
Presents an iteration dashboard using assets/dashboard-template.md format
Waits for human approval before writing to the original skill file
assertions: harness/test-inputs.md is created and contains at least 5 inputs
results.md contains a run block with baseline score
Dashboard table has at least one row per iteration
Skill file is NOT overwritten until approval is given

description: Two criteria where optimizing one risks degrading the other
prompt: Run auto-research on .claude/skills/content/SKILL.md. Criteria: (1) Hook must be under 136 characters — Level 1. (2) Hook must open with a question — Level 2. Cap at 5 iterations.
expected_behavior: Monitors both criteria every iteration, not just the one being actively optimized
If hook length improves but question format fails (or vice versa), flags the trade-off before deciding keep/discard
Does not keep a hypothesis that improves one criterion by more than 5% while degrading the other
assertions: Dashboard shows scores for BOTH criteria in every iteration row
If any criterion regresses in an iteration, the dashboard notes it explicitly

description: Skill should NOT activate when user wants to build a new skill from scratch
prompt: I want to build a new skill that helps me draft RFQ emails to suppliers. Walk me through it.
expected_behavior: auto-research does NOT activate
skill-engineer-master activates instead (or user is directed to it)
No harness or criteria design is started
assertions: No results.md run block is started
Response references skill-engineer-master or the skill-building process

description: Run with both Level 1 and Level 2 criteria — correct sub-agent routing
prompt: Run auto-research on .claude/skills/content/SKILL.md. Criteria: (1) Sentences must be 5–12 words — Level 1. (2) Post must follow PAS, IDA, or CPF writing framework — Level 2. Cap at 5 iterations.
expected_behavior: Correctly classifies criterion 1 as Level 1 and criterion 2 as Level 2
For Level 1: spawns eval-deterministic sub-agent
For Level 2: spawns eval-judge sub-agent with criterion + framework reference
Does not use the LLM judge for the deterministic criterion
Does not use the deterministic evaluator for the style criterion
assertions: Dashboard distinguishes evaluation method per criterion
Both criteria appear in every iteration row of the dashboard

description: Skill must stop and ask for reformulation if a criterion is not binary
prompt: Run auto-research on .claude/skills/content/SKILL.md. Criterion: posts should be more engaging and feel natural.
expected_behavior: Identifies that 'more engaging' and 'feel natural' are not binary conditions
Does NOT proceed to harness setup
Asks the human to reformulate as a binary condition
Suggests specific binary alternatives (e.g. 'hook must be a question', 'sentences must be under 15 words')
assertions: No harness/test-inputs.md is created before criterion approval
Response contains at least one suggested binary reformulation
